rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DAVID ACHTERKIRCHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees On Appeal Pursuant To California Civil Code Section 1717
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 1, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF DAVID ACHTERKIRCHEN Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees On Appeal Pursuant To California Civil Code Section 1717. Motion is granted in part, and denied in part. Plaintiffs' motion for attorney fees on appeal, pursuant to California Civil Code section 1717, is granted in the amount of $16,800. Although Plaintiffs did not provide copies of invoices associated with the matter, Mr. Herman's declaration outlined the specific tasks performed in the matter, the attorney who performed the tasks, the total number of hours spent on the tasks, and the attorney's hourly billing rate. (Herman Decl. paragraphs 5-6.) Plaintiffs allege that all of the tasks performed were necessary for them to succeed in the matter. Plaintiffs have provided sufficient evidence to support their claim for attorney fees in the appeal matter and Defendant has not meet his burden to show that the amount is unreasonable. Plaintiffs' motion for an award of $4,800 in attorney fees, for filing and prosecuting this motion and appearing in oral argument, is denied. The Court approves fees of $1,500 as a reasonable amount for presentation of argument in motion for attorney fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(AJR)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS LITTLE VS. DAVOL INCORPORATED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 1, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT DAVOL, INC.'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted And To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Sigrid Irias, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to deem requests for admission granted. Motion to compel responses to form interrogatories, special interrogatories, and requests for production is granted; plaintiff to provide responses without objections within 20 days of service of the court's written order. Sanctions awarded against plaintiff and in favor of defendant in the amount of $500, to be paid within 20 days of service of the court's written order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sigrid.irias@iriaslaw.com, a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: RASH B GHOSH VS. PAUL STEINER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 1, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT PAUL STEINER Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanction. Defendant's Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanctions is denied without prejudice. The plaintiff did not meet his obligations under the Discovery Act in this case. First, he did not respond to written discovery. After a motion to compel was filed, he served deficient responses. An Order from this Court required him to provide additional responses without objection. The plaintiff served additional responses which did not comply with the Court's Order. Similarly, the plaintiff did not appear for deposition as originally noticed. However, recently the plaintiff retained counsel who appears to be making strides to cure the plaintiff's discovery failings. The plaintiff sat for part of his deposition recently and the parties have scheduled additional time to complete that deposition. The plaintiff (very delinquently produced over 1,000 pages at the deposition. While the plaintiff's pro per status does not excuse his prior misdeeds, it appears premature for the extraordinary relief of terminating sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(AJR)

Pro Tem Judge Sigrid Irias, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion for relief under CCP 473(b) is denied, due to lack of a showing justifying discretionary relief under CCP 473(b), regardless of whether missing the 45-day deadline for making a motion to compel might in some circumstances qualify for relief under the statute. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK HOOSHMAND VS. KARLEEN LATORYA GRIFFIN ET AL (PLTF CHALLENGE GOLDSMITH / Deft Challenge Quinn)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff Mark Hooshmand For Limited Discovery To Oppose Anti-Slapp Motion Pursuant To Ccp 425.16(G); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration Of Mark Hooshmand
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 1, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MARK HOOSHMAND'S Motion For Limited Discovery To Oppose Anti-Slapp Motion Pursuant To CCP 425.16(G). Pro Tem Judge Sigrid Irias, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel denied. No separate statement was filed in support of the motion (CRC 3.1345). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sigrid.irias@iriaslaw.com, a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
